error.signature: CSV parser produces wrong number of fields or corrupted rows with multiline cell values

verdict.summary: RFC 4180 allows newlines inside quoted fields. Naive line-by-line CSV parsing (split on newline, then split on comma) breaks on any cell containing a newline. Python csv module handles this; pandas read_csv does too, but shell tools like awk/cut don't.

dead_ends.action: Read CSV line-by-line and split on commas
dead_ends.why_fails: Cells containing commas or newlines within quotes break this approach. A single multi-line cell becomes multiple corrupted rows.

dead_ends.action: Use awk or cut to extract CSV columns
dead_ends.why_fails: Shell tools don't understand CSV quoting. A quoted comma is treated as a delimiter. A quoted newline splits the row.

workarounds.action: Use a proper CSV parser that handles RFC 4180 (Python csv, pandas)
workarounds.how: import csv; reader = csv.reader(open('file.csv')); for row in reader: ...  # handles quoted newlines

workarounds.action: Use csvkit for shell-based CSV processing
workarounds.how: csvcut -c 1,3 file.csv  # properly handles quoting, unlike cut -d, -f1,3

workarounds.action: Pre-validate CSV with csvclean before processing
workarounds.how: csvclean file.csv  # reports and optionally fixes quoting issues
